one_time_description: Retail sales from newly opened cannabis and related products dispensary (operating without provincial licence, noted as non-compliant with Cannabis Act)
economic_notes: First Nation opened an unlicensed cannabis retail operation during the year. Received Enbridge Community Investment of $379,500 and Line 3 Pipeline premiums of $217,844. Has a TLE (Treaty Land Entitlement) claim with the federal government that could result in significant cash inflow. Oil and gas royalties received from lease agreements on designated reserve land.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Nation operates an unlicensed cannabis retail store flagged as non-compliant with the Cannabis Act by auditor in Emphasis of Matter paragraph (audit opinion not modified). One consolidated subsidiary: Pheasant Rump Nakota First Nation CMHC Housing. Yorkton Tribal Council Child and Family Services Inc. is a related organization not consolidated. Significant revenue from Enbridge/Line 3 pipeline agreements ($597,344 combined). TLE land claim pending with probable but unquantified cash inflow. Two CMHC Phase 3 and Phase 4 housing construction loans totalling $423,082 with terms not yet set. Subsequent event: approved for $357,857 CMHC Section 95 loan for two additional housing units. osr calculated as total revenue $7,245,858 minus ISC $3,708,495 minus CMHC $11,004 = $3,526,359.
